description: Girsanov / change-of-measure / Black-Scholes finance theorems from Saporito Ch 9

theorems.name: Novikov's Condition
theorems.description: Theorem 9.1.7: If E[exp((1/2) ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶÄ őł_s¬≤ ds)] < ‚ąě, then the Doleans-Dade exponential Z_t = exp(‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó őł_s dB_s - (1/2) ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó őł_s¬≤ ds) is a true martingale on [0,T]

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Novikov-condition specification: a progressively measurable integrand őł
    on `[0, T]` with `E[exp((1/2) ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶÄ őł_s¬≤ ds)] < ‚ąě` and a witness that the
    Dol√©ans‚ÄďDade exponential `Z_t = exp(‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó őł_s dB_s ‚ąí (1/2) ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó őł_s¬≤ ds)`
    is a true martingale on `[0, T]` (Theorem 9.1.7). -/
structure NovikovCondition {ő© : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace ő©]
    (őľ : Measure ő©) [IsProbabilityMeasure őľ] (ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ m0)
    (T : ‚ĄĚ) (Z : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) : Prop where
  T_pos : 0 < T
  /-- Stand-in for the Novikov hypothesis: a uniform L¬Ļ bound on `Z`. The
      genuine condition `E[exp(¬Ĺ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶÄ őł¬≤ ds)] < ‚ąě` needs the stochastic integral
      `‚ąę őł dB` (no `őł` or `B` appears in this structure) and is not encoded. -/
  novikov_finite : ‚ąÉ R : ‚ĄĚ, ‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc (0 : ‚ĄĚ) T,
    eLpNorm (Z t) 1 őľ ‚Č§ ENNReal.ofReal R
  /-- Theorem 9.1.7: `Z` is a martingale on `[0, T]`. -/
  is_martingale : Martingale Z ūĚďē őľ

/-- Theorem 9.1.7 (Novikov's condition): under the Novikov integrability
    condition, the Dol√©ans‚ÄďDade exponential `Z` is a true martingale on `[0, T]`. -/
theorem NovikovCondition.doleansDade_is_martingale
    {ő© : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace ő©] {őľ : Measure ő©} [IsProbabilityMeasure őľ]
    {ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ m0} {T : ‚ĄĚ} {Z : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ}
    (h : NovikovCondition őľ ūĚďē T Z) :
    Martingale Z ūĚďē őľ :=
  h.is_martingale

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean specification (structure spec): the structure carries `T_pos`, a uniform L¬Ļ bound on `Z` standing in for the Novikov integrability hypothesis ‚ÄĒ the genuine condition E[exp(¬Ĺ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶÄ őł¬≤ ds)] < ‚ąě requires the stochastic integral ‚ąęőł dB, and no őł or B appears in the structure ‚ÄĒ and the conclusion `Martingale Z ūĚďē őľ` as a field; the theorem reads off the conclusion via a structural projection. This is statement-level encoding, not a derivation. Marked `reduced_core` to remain honest: a real proof requires the It√ī-integral / Dol√©ans‚ÄďDade machinery currently outside Mathlib at the pinned toolchain.

theorems.name: Girsanov: Drifted BM under Equivalent Measure
theorems.description: Theorem 9.1.8 (1st version): For bounded őł, B^őł_t = B_t - ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó őł_s ds is a Brownian motion under the measure dP^őł/dP = Z_T

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Girsanov-theorem specification (1st version): a bounded drift `őł`, a
    P-Brownian motion `B`, the Dol√©ans‚ÄďDade exponential `Z_T`, and the equivalent
    probability measure `Q = Z_T ¬∑ P` under which the drift-corrected process
    `B^őł_t = B_t ‚ąí ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó őł_s ds` is a Brownian motion (Theorem 9.1.8). -/
structure GirsanovDriftBM {ő© : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ő©]
    (P : Measure ő©) [IsProbabilityMeasure P] (T : ‚ĄĚ) (B Bőł : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)
    (őł : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (Z_T : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) where
  T_pos : 0 < T
  /-- B is a P-Brownian motion (zero start, Gaussian indep increments). -/
  zero_start : ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāP, B 0 ŌČ = 0
  gaussian_increments : ‚ąÄ ‚¶És t : ‚ĄĚ‚¶Ą, s ‚Č§ t ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ v : NNReal, (v : ‚ĄĚ) = t - s ‚ąß
      Measure.map (fun ŌČ => B t ŌČ - B s ŌČ) P = gaussianReal 0 v
  independent_increments : ‚ąÄ ‚¶És t u v : ‚ĄĚ‚¶Ą, s ‚Č§ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ u ‚Üí u ‚Č§ v ‚Üí
    IndepFun (fun ŌČ => B t ŌČ - B s ŌČ) (fun ŌČ => B v ŌČ - B u ŌČ) P
  /-- őł is uniformly bounded. -/
  theta_bounded : ‚ąÉ K : ‚ĄĚ, ‚ąÄ t ŌČ, |őł t ŌČ| ‚Č§ K
  /-- Definition of the drift-corrected process Bőł. -/
  Bőł_def : ‚ąÄ t ŌČ, Bőł t ŌČ = B t ŌČ - ‚ąę s in (0 : ‚ĄĚ)..t, őł s ŌČ
  /-- Z_T is positive a.s. (P) and has unit P-mean. -/
  Z_pos : ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāP, 0 < Z_T ŌČ
  Z_mean_one : ‚ąę ŌČ, Z_T ŌČ ‚ąāP = 1
  /-- Theorem 9.1.8: under the equivalent measure Q with density Z_T,
      the drift-corrected process Bőł has Gaussian increments of variance t ‚ąí s
      independent across disjoint intervals ‚ÄĒ i.e., it is a Q-Brownian motion. -/
  Q : Measure ő©
  Q_isProbability : IsProbabilityMeasure Q
  Q_density : Q = P.withDensity (fun ŌČ => ENNReal.ofReal (Z_T ŌČ))
  Bőł_Q_brownian :
    (‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāQ, Bőł 0 ŌČ = 0) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ ‚¶És t : ‚ĄĚ‚¶Ą, s ‚Č§ t ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ v : NNReal, (v : ‚ĄĚ) = t - s ‚ąß
        Measure.map (fun ŌČ => Bőł t ŌČ - Bőł s ŌČ) Q = gaussianReal 0 v) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ ‚¶És t u v : ‚ĄĚ‚¶Ą, s ‚Č§ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ u ‚Üí u ‚Č§ v ‚Üí
      IndepFun (fun ŌČ => Bőł t ŌČ - Bőł s ŌČ) (fun ŌČ => Bőł v ŌČ - Bőł u ŌČ) Q)

/-- Theorem 9.1.8 (Girsanov, 1st version): under the equivalent measure `Q` with
    density `Z_T`, the drift-corrected process `B^őł_t = B_t ‚ąí ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó őł_s ds` is a
    Brownian motion (zero start, Gaussian independent increments). -/
theorem GirsanovDriftBM.driftCorrected_is_QBrownian
    {ő© : Type*} [MeasurableSpace ő©] {P : Measure ő©} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {T : ‚ĄĚ} {B Bőł : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ} {őł : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ} {Z_T : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ}
    (h : GirsanovDriftBM P T B Bőł őł Z_T) :
    (‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāh.Q, Bőł 0 ŌČ = 0) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ ‚¶És t : ‚ĄĚ‚¶Ą, s ‚Č§ t ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ v : NNReal, (v : ‚ĄĚ) = t - s ‚ąß
        Measure.map (fun ŌČ => Bőł t ŌČ - Bőł s ŌČ) h.Q = gaussianReal 0 v) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ ‚¶És t u v : ‚ĄĚ‚¶Ą, s ‚Č§ t ‚Üí t ‚Č§ u ‚Üí u ‚Č§ v ‚Üí
      IndepFun (fun ŌČ => Bőł t ŌČ - Bőł s ŌČ) (fun ŌČ => Bőł v ŌČ - Bőł u ŌČ) h.Q) :=
  h.Bőł_Q_brownian

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean specification: the structure encodes the textbook hypotheses and the textbook conclusion as fields, so any inhabitant satisfies the textbook statement by construction; the theorem reads off the conclusion via a structural projection. This is statement-level encoding, not a derivation of the textbook conclusion from foundational primitives. Marked `reduced_core` to remain honest: a real proof requires the underlying process / measure-theoretic infrastructure currently outside Mathlib at the pinned toolchain.

theorems.name: Black-Scholes Call Pricing Formula
theorems.description: Black-Scholes call price (Ch 9.4): C(S,t) = S ő¶(d_1) - K e^{-r(T-t)} ő¶(d_2) where d_1 = (log(S/K) + (r + ŌÉ¬≤/2)(T-t))/(ŌÉ ‚ąö(T-t)) and d_2 = d_1 - ŌÉ ‚ąö(T-t)

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.Call

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal ENNReal
open MathFin

variable {ő© : Type*} {mő© : MeasurableSpace ő©}

/-- Theorem (Saporito Ch 9.4) ‚ÄĒ Black-Scholes European call pricing formula.

For an asset whose risk-neutral log-return is Gaussian (the standard BS
hypothesis), the discounted expected payoff of the European call
`max(S_T - K, 0)` equals `S_0 ¬∑ ő¶(d_1) ‚ąí K ¬∑ e^{-rT} ¬∑ ő¶(d_2)`, where:
  d_1 = (log(S_0/K) + (r + ŌÉ¬≤/2)T) / (ŌÉ‚ąöT)
  d_2 = d_1 ‚ąí ŌÉ‚ąöT

Wraps `MathFin.bs_call_formula`, which derives this from primitives:
HasLaw transfer to standard normal, max ‚Üí indicator on exercise region
`{z : S_T(z) > K} = Set.Ioi(-d_2)`, completing-the-square integral
`‚ąę z in Ioi(-d_2), exp(ŌÉ‚ąöT¬∑z) ¬∑ pdf(0,1,z) = exp(ŌÉ¬≤T/2) ¬∑ ő¶(d_1)`, and
the BS martingale algebra `(r ‚ąí ŌÉ¬≤/2)T + ŌÉ¬≤T/2 = rT`.

No upstream It√ī calculus / Girsanov required; pure Gaussian integration.
Axioms-clean per `#print axioms`: depends only on `propext`,
`Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`. -/
theorem black_scholes_call_formula
    {Q : Measure ő©} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r ŌÉ T : ‚ĄĚ} {Z : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r ŌÉ T Z) :
    ‚ąę ŌČ, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (bsTerminal S_0 r ŌÉ T (Z ŌČ) - K) 0 ‚ąāQ
      = S_0 * Phi (bsd1 S_0 K r ŌÉ T) -
        K * Real.exp (-r * T) * Phi (bsd2 S_0 K r ŌÉ T) :=
  MathFin.bs_call_formula h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof of the Black-Scholes European call pricing formula. Wraps MathFin.bs_call_formula, which derives E_Q[e^{-rT} max(S_T - K, 0)] = S_0 Phi(d_1) - K e^{-rT} Phi(d_2) from primitives: HasLaw.integral_comp (transfer ‚ąę_Q to ‚ąę_(gaussianReal 0 1)), integral_gaussianReal_eq_integral_smul (convert to ‚ąę with gaussianPDFReal), max_payoff_eq_indicator + integral_indicator (restrict to {z > -d_2}), integral_exp_mul_gaussianPDFReal_Ioi (completing-the-square, ‚ąę exp(ŌÉ‚ąöT z) pdf(0,1,z) = exp(ŌÉ¬≤T/2) Phi(d_1)), gaussianReal_Ioi_toReal (‚ąę pdf = Phi(d_2)), and the BS martingale algebra (r-ŌÉ¬≤/2)T + ŌÉ¬≤T/2 = rT. Lives at MathFin/BlackScholes/Call.lean (~340 lines, Phi infrastructure + completing-the-square + shifted-tail integral + main theorem). No upstream It√ī calculus / Girsanov machinery required ‚ÄĒ pure Gaussian integration. Axioms-clean per #print axioms: depends only on propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

theorems.name: Continuous-Time First FTAP: Discounted GBM is a Martingale
theorems.description: Under the risk-neutral measure, the discounted geometric Brownian motion price e^{‚ąírt}S_t is a martingale with respect to the Brownian filtration ‚ÄĒ the defining property of the equivalent martingale measure and the operational content of the first fundamental theorem of asset pricing in continuous time.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ContinuousFTAP

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal

/-- Continuous-time first FTAP, martingale property: under the risk-neutral measure,
    the discounted Black‚ÄďScholes price `e^{‚ąírt}¬∑S_t`, with
    `S_t = S‚āÄ¬∑exp((r ‚ąí ŌÉ¬≤/2)t + ŌÉX_t)` driven by a filtered pre-Brownian motion `X`,
    is a martingale w.r.t. the Brownian filtration ‚ÄĒ the defining property of the
    equivalent martingale measure. Re-export of `MathFin.discountedGBM_isMartingale`
    (`MathFin/Foundations/ContinuousFTAP.lean`: the discounted price IS `S‚āÄ` times
    the Wald exponential `exp(ŌÉX_t ‚ąí ŌÉ¬≤t/2)`, a martingale by
    `IsFilteredPreBrownian.waldExponential_isMartingale`). -/
theorem discounted_gbm_is_Q_martingale
    {ő© : Type*} {mő© : MeasurableSpace ő©} {P : Measure ő©}
    {ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 mő©} {X : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ}
    [IsFilteredPreBrownian X ūĚďē P] [IsFiniteMeasure P] (S_0 r ŌÉ : ‚ĄĚ) :
    Martingale (fun (t : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0) ŌČ => Real.exp (-r * t) *
        (S_0 * Real.exp ((r - ŌÉ ^ 2 / 2) * t + ŌÉ * X t ŌČ))) ūĚďē P :=
  MathFin.discountedGBM_isMartingale S_0 r ŌÉ

metadata.reference: Risk-neutral pricing / first FTAP, continuous time (Saporito Ch 9; Shreve II Ch 5)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. The discounted price e^{‚ąírt}¬∑S_t = S‚āÄ¬∑exp(ŌÉX_t ‚ąí ŌÉ¬≤t/2) is S‚āÄ ‚ÄĘ (Wald exponential), and IsFilteredPreBrownian.waldExponential_isMartingale (MathFin/Foundations/BrownianMartingale.lean: pull-out + condExp_indep_eq + Gaussian MGF) plus Martingale.smul give the martingale property ‚ÄĒ no further stochastic-calculus machinery. Continuous-time analogue of the discrete binomial EMM (Binomial/SecondFTAP.lean). Lives in MathFin/Foundations/ContinuousFTAP.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Bayes Change-of-Measure Martingale Engine
theorems.description: The abstract kernel of Girsanov's theorem: if the density process Z and the product Z¬∑D are both P-martingales and Z_T ‚Č• 0, then D is a martingale on [0,T] under the tilted measure Q = withDensity(Z_T). No stochastic calculus ‚ÄĒ only conditional expectations (a Bayes pull-out and a martingale set-integral). The reusable engine consumed by the Black-Scholes Girsanov instantiation.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ChangeOfMeasure

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal

/-- **Bayes change-of-measure martingale engine** (Girsanov's abstract kernel). If `Z`
    and the product `Z ¬∑ D` are both `P`-martingales, `D` is adapted, and `Z_T ‚Č• 0` is a
    density, then `D` is a martingale on `[0, T]` under `Q = P.withDensity Z_T`: for
    `s ‚Č§ t ‚Č§ T` and `A ‚ąą ūĚďē_s` the `Q`-integrals of `D_t` and `D_s` over `A` agree. No
    stochastic calculus ‚ÄĒ only conditional expectations (a Bayes pull-out plus a
    martingale set-integral). Re-exports `MathFin.changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq`. -/
theorem change_of_measure_martingale
    {ő© : Type*} {mő© : MeasurableSpace ő©} {P : Measure ő©} [IsFiniteMeasure P]
    {ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 mő©} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P ūĚďē]
    {Z D : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ} (T : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0)
    (hZmeasT : Measurable (Z T)) (hZpos : ‚ąÄ ŌČ, 0 ‚Č§ Z T ŌČ)
    (hDsm : ‚ąÄ u, StronglyMeasurable[ūĚďē u] (D u))
    (hZ : Martingale Z ūĚďē P)
    (hZD : Martingale (fun t ŌČ => Z t ŌČ * D t ŌČ) ūĚďē P)
    (hmix : ‚ąÄ u, u ‚Č§ T ‚Üí Integrable (fun ŌČ => D u ŌČ * Z T ŌČ) P)
    {s t : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0} (hst : s ‚Č§ t) (htT : t ‚Č§ T)
    {A : Set ő©} (hA : MeasurableSet[ūĚďē s] A) :
    ‚ąę ŌČ in A, D t ŌČ ‚ąā(P.withDensity (fun ŌČ => ENNReal.ofReal (Z T ŌČ)))
      = ‚ąę ŌČ in A, D s ŌČ ‚ąā(P.withDensity (fun ŌČ => ENNReal.ofReal (Z T ŌČ))) :=
  MathFin.changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq T hZmeasT hZpos hDsm hZ hZD hmix hst htT hA

metadata.reference: Girsanov / abstract change of measure (Saporito Ch 9; Shreve II Ch 5.2)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof re-exported from MathFin/Foundations/ChangeOfMeasure.lean. For Q = P.withDensity(ENNReal.ofReal Z_T), the identity ‚ąę_A D_t dQ = ‚ąę_A Z_t D_t dP = ‚ąę_A Z_s D_s dP = ‚ąę_A D_s dQ. The outer equalities are the Bayes pull-out: for A ‚ąą ūĚďē_s ‚äÜ ūĚďē_u and D_u ūĚďē_u-measurable, ‚ąę_A D_u Z_T dP = ‚ąę_A D_u¬∑P[Z_T|ūĚďē_u] dP = ‚ąę_A D_u Z_u dP by the Z-martingale property (condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left + setIntegral_condExp); the middle is the Z¬∑D martingale set-integral (Martingale.setIntegral_eq). withDensity conversion via setIntegral_withDensity_eq_setIntegral_toReal_smul. Axioms-clean: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

theorems.name: Black-Scholes EMM via an Explicit Girsanov Change of Measure
theorems.description: The Black-Scholes risk-neutral measure constructed as a Girsanov density change of the physical measure. Under physical drift őľ the discounted stock D_t = S_0¬∑exp((őľ‚ąír‚ąíŌÉ¬≤/2)t + ŌÉX_t) is only a submartingale; tilting P by the Girsanov density exp(‚ąíőłX_T ‚ąí ¬Ĺőł¬≤T) with market price of risk őł = (őľ‚ąír)/ŌÉ turns it into a martingale on [0,T] under Q = withDensity(Z_T). Q is the equivalent martingale measure, exhibited as an explicit change of measure ‚ÄĒ retiring the Wald shortcut of the continuous FTAP. The martingale side of the I‚ÜĒII (pricing‚ÜĒIt√ī) seam; the distributional Girsanov (B^őł = B ‚ąí ‚ąęőł ds is Q-Brownian, gir-thm-9.1.8) needs an adapted-integrand It√ī formula and remains open.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.Girsanov

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal

/-- **Black‚ÄďScholes EMM as an explicit Girsanov change of measure.** With physical drift
    `őľ`, rate `r`, volatility `ŌÉ ‚Č† 0`, and market price of risk `őł = (őľ ‚ąí r)/ŌÉ`, the
    discounted stock `D_t = S_0¬∑exp((őľ ‚ąí r ‚ąí ŌÉ¬≤/2)t + ŌÉX_t)` is a martingale on `[0, T]`
    under the tilted measure `Q = P.withDensity(exp(‚ąíőłX_T ‚ąí ¬Ĺőł¬≤T))` ‚ÄĒ the risk-neutral
    measure constructed as a Girsanov density change, retiring the Wald shortcut. The
    proof feeds two Wald exponentials (`Z`, `Z¬∑D`) to the change-of-measure engine, with
    mixed-time integrability of `D_u¬∑Z_T` via AM‚ÄďGM. Re-exports
    `MathFin.bs_discounted_isQMartingale`. -/
theorem bs_emm_via_girsanov
    {ő© : Type*} {mő© : MeasurableSpace ő©} {P : Measure ő©} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 mő©} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P ūĚďē]
    {X : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ} [IsFilteredPreBrownian X ūĚďē P]
    (S_0 r őľ ŌÉ : ‚ĄĚ) (hŌÉ : ŌÉ ‚Č† 0) (T : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0)
    {s t : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0} (hst : s ‚Č§ t) (htT : t ‚Č§ T)
    {A : Set ő©} (hA : MeasurableSet[ūĚďē s] A) :
    ‚ąę ŌČ in A, (S_0 * Real.exp ((őľ - r - ŌÉ ^ 2 / 2) * (t : ‚ĄĚ) + ŌÉ * X t ŌČ))
        ‚ąā(P.withDensity fun ŌČ => ENNReal.ofReal
          (Real.exp (-((őľ - r) / ŌÉ) * X T ŌČ - ((őľ - r) / ŌÉ) ^ 2 * (T : ‚ĄĚ) / 2)))
      = ‚ąę ŌČ in A, (S_0 * Real.exp ((őľ - r - ŌÉ ^ 2 / 2) * (s : ‚ĄĚ) + ŌÉ * X s ŌČ))
        ‚ąā(P.withDensity fun ŌČ => ENNReal.ofReal
          (Real.exp (-((őľ - r) / ŌÉ) * X T ŌČ - ((őľ - r) / ŌÉ) ^ 2 * (T : ‚ĄĚ) / 2))) :=
  MathFin.bs_discounted_isQMartingale S_0 r őľ ŌÉ hŌÉ T hst htT hA

metadata.reference: Girsanov change of measure / risk-neutral pricing (Saporito Ch 9; Shreve II Ch 5.2-5.4)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof re-exported from MathFin/Foundations/Girsanov.lean. Instantiates the change-of-measure engine (changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq) with two Wald exponentials: the Girsanov density Z = exp(‚ąíőłX ‚ąí ¬Ĺőł¬≤¬∑) = waldExponential(‚ąíőł) and the product Z¬∑D = S_0¬∑exp((ŌÉ‚ąíőł)X ‚ąí ¬Ĺ(ŌÉ‚ąíőł)¬≤¬∑) = S_0¬∑waldExponential(ŌÉ‚ąíőł), both P-martingales by IsFilteredPreBrownian.waldExponential_isMartingale (using őľ‚ąír = ŌÉőł). Adaptedness of D via continuous-comp of the adapted X_u; the one new estimate is the mixed-time integrability of D_u¬∑Z_T, handled by AM-GM exp(ŌÉX_u)exp(‚ąíőłX_T) ‚Č§ exp(2ŌÉX_u)+exp(‚ąí2őłX_T), each Gaussian-MGF-integrable (integrable_exp_mul_of_hasLaw). Does NOT claim the distributional Girsanov (drift-corrected process is Q-Brownian). Axioms-clean: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

theorems.name: Martingale Representation Theorem
theorems.description: Corollary 9.3.4: Every square-integrable martingale M adapted to the BM filtration can be written M_t = M_0 + ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó Ōē_s dB_s for some Ōē ‚ąą H¬≤

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Martingale-representation specification on the Brownian filtration: every
    square-integrable martingale `M` adapted to the BM filtration on `[0, T]`
    has an integrand `ŌÜ ‚ąą H¬≤` and an initial value `M_0` with
    `M_t = M_0 + ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó ŌÜ_s dB_s` (Corollary 9.3.4). -/
structure MartingaleRepresentation {ő© : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace ő©]
    (őľ : Measure ő©) [IsProbabilityMeasure őľ]
    (ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ m0) (B : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (T : ‚ĄĚ)
    (M : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) : Prop where
  T_pos : 0 < T
  /-- M is a martingale on [0, T]. -/
  is_martingale : Martingale M ūĚďē őľ
  /-- M is square-integrable on [0, T]. -/
  M_squareIntegrable : ‚ąÉ R : ‚ĄĚ, ‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc (0 : ‚ĄĚ) T,
    eLpNorm (M t) 2 őľ ‚Č§ ENNReal.ofReal R
  /-- Corollary 9.3.4: there exists an H¬≤-integrand ŌÜ and an initial value M‚āÄ
      such that the stochastic-integral representation
      `M_t = M‚āÄ + ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó ŌÜ_s dB_s` holds ‚ÄĒ encoded as the existence of an
      integrand process `ŌÜ` and an initial value `M‚āÄ` together with the
      pointwise representation of `M` as the corresponding stochastic integral. -/
  representation : ‚ąÉ (ŌÜ : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (M0 : ‚ĄĚ) (stochInt : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ),
    (‚ąÄ R : ‚ĄĚ, 0 ‚Č§ R ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ CŌÜ : ‚ĄĚ, ‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc (0 : ‚ĄĚ) T,
        eLpNorm (ŌÜ t) 2 őľ ‚Č§ ENNReal.ofReal CŌÜ) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ t ŌČ, M t ŌČ = M0 + stochInt t ŌČ)

/-- Corollary 9.3.4 (martingale representation theorem): every square-
    integrable martingale on the Brownian filtration has an integrand
    `ŌÜ ‚ąą H¬≤` and an initial value `M‚āÄ` such that `M_t = M‚āÄ + ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó ŌÜ_s dB_s`. -/
theorem MartingaleRepresentation.exists_predictable_integrand
    {ő© : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace ő©] {őľ : Measure ő©} [IsProbabilityMeasure őľ]
    {ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ m0} {B : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ} {T : ‚ĄĚ} {M : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ}
    (h : MartingaleRepresentation őľ ūĚďē B T M) :
    ‚ąÉ (ŌÜ : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (M0 : ‚ĄĚ) (stochInt : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ),
    (‚ąÄ R : ‚ĄĚ, 0 ‚Č§ R ‚Üí
      ‚ąÉ CŌÜ : ‚ĄĚ, ‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc (0 : ‚ĄĚ) T,
        eLpNorm (ŌÜ t) 2 őľ ‚Č§ ENNReal.ofReal CŌÜ) ‚ąß
    (‚ąÄ t ŌČ, M t ŌČ = M0 + stochInt t ŌČ) :=
  h.representation

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean specification: the structure encodes the textbook hypotheses and the textbook conclusion as fields, so any inhabitant satisfies the textbook statement by construction; the theorem reads off the conclusion via a structural projection. This is statement-level encoding, not a derivation of the textbook conclusion from foundational primitives. Marked `reduced_core` to remain honest: a real proof requires the underlying process / measure-theoretic infrastructure currently outside Mathlib at the pinned toolchain.

theorems.name: Constant-őł Distributional Girsanov: Marginal Law
theorems.description: Under the constant market-price-of-risk Girsanov measure Q = P.withDensity(exp(‚ąíőł X_T ‚ąí ¬Ĺőł¬≤ T)), the drift-corrected process B^őł_t = X_t + őł t has marginal law N(0, t) ‚ÄĒ the distributional side of the I‚ÜĒII Girsanov seam, at the marginal level, for constant őł, derived from the existing tower without an adapted-integrand It√ī formula.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.GirsanovConstantTheta

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal
open MathFin

/-- Constant-őł distributional Girsanov (marginal law). Under the Girsanov measure
    `Q = P.withDensity(exp(‚ąíőł X_T ‚ąí ¬Ĺőł¬≤ T))`, the drift-corrected process
    `B^őł_t = X_t + őł t` has law `gaussianReal 0 t = N(0, t)`. Re-exports
    `MathFin.Btheta_map_eq_gaussianReal`, derived from primitives: the Bayes
    change-of-measure engine plus two Wald exponentials give the `Q`-MGF `exp(¬Ĺ t a¬≤)`,
    and Mathlib's complex-MGF machinery (`integrableExpSet_eq_of_mgf`,
    `eqOn_complexMGF_of_mgf`, `Measure.ext_of_complexMGF_eq`) reads off the Gaussian law.
    No adapted-integrand It√ī formula required. -/
theorem girsanov_const_theta_marginal_law
    {ő© : Type*} {mő© : MeasurableSpace ő©} {P : Measure ő©} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {ūĚďē : Filtration ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 mő©} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P ūĚďē]
    {X : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0 ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ} [IsFilteredPreBrownian X ūĚďē P]
    (őł : ‚ĄĚ) (T : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0) {t : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0} (htT : t ‚Č§ T) :
    (P.withDensity fun ŌČ => ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-őł * X T ŌČ - őł ^ 2 * (T : ‚ĄĚ) / 2))).map
        (fun ŌČ => X t ŌČ + őł * (t : ‚ĄĚ))
      = gaussianReal 0 t :=
  MathFin.Btheta_map_eq_gaussianReal (ūĚďē := ūĚďē) őł T htT

metadata.reference: Girsanov, distributional side (constant őł, marginal law)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof (constant őł, marginal level). Re-exports MathFin.Btheta_map_eq_gaussianReal (MathFin/Foundations/GirsanovConstantTheta.lean): under Q = P.withDensity(exp(‚ąíőł X_T ‚ąí ¬Ĺőł¬≤ T)) the drift-corrected marginal B^őł_t = X_t + őł t has law N(0, t). Derived from primitives ‚ÄĒ the Bayes change-of-measure engine (changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq) fed two Wald exponentials Wald(‚ąíőł), Wald(a‚ąíőł) gives the Q-MGF exp(¬Ĺ t a¬≤); Mathlib's complex-MGF machinery (integrableExpSet_eq_of_mgf transfers the full-line integrable-exponential set from the Gaussian, eqOn_complexMGF_of_mgf upgrades the real-MGF match to a complex-MGF match on ‚Ąā, Measure.ext_of_complexMGF_eq reads off the law). No adapted-integrand It√ī formula required. This is the constant-őł, marginal half of the distributional Girsanov theorem ‚ÄĒ the general bounded-őł full Q-Brownian (gir-thm-9.1.8) remains reduced_core: its increment independence needs a conditional-MGF ‚üĻ independence result absent from Mathlib at the pin. Axioms-clean.
